ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301099 covers tea beverage broadly; no exact code for packaged flavoured unsweetened RTD tea. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers tea and tea-based beverages; no sub-code specific to packaged flavoured unsweetened RTD tea. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for packaged flavoured unsweetened RTD tea.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve bottle or can of packaged flavoured tea is approximately 250 ml (250 g), consumed as a ready-to-drink beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food — Flavoured Tea (No Sugar), HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient database, which is the approved primary regional source for Singapore food records under Project Veritas v2 source policy. The input record was flagged as reviewed with 0.95 confidence from HPB. All core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate) and secondary nutrients (sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are confirmed from this HPB Foodo entry for flavoured tea with no added sugar, packaged.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates; GI concept not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is not applicable as this beverage contains 0 g carbohydrates per 100 g per HPB Singapore Foodo database. No glycaemic response is expected from a zero-carbohydrate beverage. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by calculation (GL = GI x carbohydrate_g_per_serving / 100; carbohydrate_g_per_serving = 0 g, therefore GL = 0.0). Per the University of Sydney GI Database methodology, GI testing requires a food to deliver 50 g available carbohydrate, which is not possible for this product; GI is therefore classified as not_applicable rather than assigned a numeric value. glycemic_index field is set to null (not 0) in accordance with the Veritas zero-carbohydrate GI rule.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (2 mg per 100 g) and zero sugar
hypertension.guidance: With only 2 mg sodium per 100 g and no sugar, this packaged unsweetened flavoured tea poses negligible blood pressure risk and is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension, provided the product label confirms no hidden sodium or sweeteners.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugar; no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage contains 0 g carbohydrates and 0 g sugar per 100 g, producing no glycaemic response, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, consumers should verify the product label to confirm the absence of added sugars, fruit juice concentrates, or artificial sweeteners.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, very low sodium, and tea polyphenols
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains no fat, no saturated fat, and only 2 mg sodium per 100 g; tea polyphenols (catechins and flavonoids) present in genuine tea extract have been associated with modest cardioprotective effects in peer-reviewed literature, making this a heart-friendly beverage choice when consumed as part of a balanced diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Tea polyphenols with potential anti-inflammatory properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Tea polyphenols (catechins, flavonoids) present in genuine tea extract have documented anti-inflammatory properties that may be mildly beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis; however, consumers should confirm that genuine tea extract rather than purely synthetic flavouring is used, and monitor caffeine intake when consuming multiple servings alongside RA medications.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured Tea (No Sugar, Packaged) is a ready-to-drink (RTD) commercial beverage consisting primarily of water, tea extract, and natural or artificial flavourings, with no added sugar or caloric sweeteners. It is sold in sealed bottles or cans in single-serve or multi-serve formats and requires no preparation before consumption. The beverage is essentially calorie-free, containing 0 kcal energy, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrates, and 0 g sugar per 100 g, with only a trace of sodium at 2 mg per 100 g. The flavour profile varies by product variant and may include fruit (lemon, peach, lychee, passionfruit), floral (jasmine, rose), herbal (mint, chamomile), or spice notes, but the nutritional profile remains consistent at near-zero across all macronutrients regardless of flavour. The texture is light and watery, similar to brewed tea, with a mild to moderate flavour intensity depending on the brand and flavour variant. From a clinical perspective, this beverage is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (zero carbohydrates, no glycaemic impact), hypertension (very low sodium at 2 mg per 100 g), and cardiovascular disease (no fat, no saturated fat, trace sodium). Tea polyphenols such as catechins and flavonoids, if genuine tea extract is used, may confer modest antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits supported by peer-reviewed research. This product is appropriate for vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free dietary patterns, and is compatible with ketogenic and low-calorie dietary approaches. It is commonly consumed as a refreshing low-calorie alternative to sugary beverages and is widely available in Singapore and across Southeast Asia in convenience stores, supermarkets, and vending machines.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database, which is the approved primary regional source for Singapore food records under Project Veritas v2 source policy. The input record was flagged as reviewed with 0.95 confidence from HPB. All core macros and secondary nutrients are confirmed from this HPB Foodo entry.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero as this is a water-based tea beverage with no protein-contributing ingredients. fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation — no fat-containing ingredients are present. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database entry; the product contains no added sugars, syrups, or starch. energy_kcal is naturally zero consistent with zero macronutrients. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g per 100 g); glycemic_index set to null (not 0) and gi_category set to 'not_applicable' per the Veritas zero-carbohydrate GI rule. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this packaged flavoured unsweetened tea category are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry as of 2026. calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 per HPB Foodo source. The RA health suitability entry from the input has been preserved and reclassified as 'low concern' with corrected reasoning: tea polyphenols may provide mild anti-inflammatory benefit, but the food is not actively contraindicated for RA. Dietary tags from input (low_sodium, low_calorie, keto) are supported by the nutritional profile. This record covers the generic category for any flavour variant of packaged no-sugar flavoured tea; individual product formulations may vary and label verification is recommended.
